content:
type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Can't split at that granularity - graded relevance requires the idcg block itself to use relevance_scores (ideal ranking = expected_ids sorted by score descending, using graded rel in discounted_gain), not just the ideal_length fix. So the idcg block is intrinsically part of my change too. To avoid us clobbering the same lines, let me own the whole NDCG.compute method and I will apply your exact fix as the base case: ideal_length/loop will use len(expected_ids) (not min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))) when relevance_scores is None, exactly as you specified. Then when relevance_scores IS provided, idcg sums over expected_ids sorted by score desc. Does that satisfy your fix's intent? If yes, reply ACCEPT with my_files=metrics.py for me, and you can take DONE without further edits to this file (or point me to any other file if your task has one).
